title: Oregano infusion for a chest cold
subtitle: An expectorant cup for productive cough
excerpt: A hot infusion of dried oregano, steeped covered and drunk warm. Traditionally taken for a productive cough with chest congestion during a cold.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve (the early 20th-century botanical writer), A Modern Herbal, marjoram/oregano entry: the expectorant and warming respiratory tradition. The same preparation appears in Greek and Spanish folk medicine records. the World Health Organisation region-specific traditional use documentation for Origanum vulgare in respiratory preparations.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Dried oregano stores 12 months in a sealed tin. Prepare each cup fresh.

glossaryTerms.definition: A herb that helps loosen and move mucus from the airways. Oregano's expectorant action is associated with the volatile oil constituents carvacrol and thymol.

content.text: Oregano (Origanum vulgare) is a kitchen herb with a long place in European herbal medicine for coughs and chest colds. Its aromatic oil contains carvacrol and thymol, the same active compounds as thyme. It has a traditional use as an

for coughs with chest congestion during a cold. The preparation is simple: steep one teaspoon of dried oregano in a covered teapot for 10 minutes. The lid matters: the aromatic oil evaporates easily as steam. The infusion is pleasant and gentler on the stomach than thyme.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Pregnancy. Culinary oregano in cooking is safe throughout pregnancy. Daily therapeutic infusions (several cups per day over a week or more) at a medicinal dose go beyond the culinary context; consult your midwife before therapeutic use in pregnancy.

Lamiaceae allergy. Oregano is in the mint family (Lamiaceae). Readers with a known sensitivity to other Lamiaceae plants (sage, thyme, lavender) should approach with caution.

Coughs that need medical attention. A cough with blood, significant breathlessness, a productive cough with high fever for more than 3 days, or a cough lasting more than 3 weeks needs medical assessment.

items.prepNote: about 1 teaspoon of dried oregano; fresh oregano can be used (double the quantity)

content.text: Place the oregano in a teapot with a lid. Pour over the just-boiled water. Put the lid on immediately and steep for 10 minutes. The infusion should smell strongly aromatic and look pale gold or pale green. Strain into a cup. Drink warm. If using honey, add after straining, not during the steep.

One cup (250 ml, containing 2 g dried oregano) up to three times a day during an acute chest cold. Use for the duration of the acute episode, typically 5-7 days. If symptoms are not improving after 7 days, or if they worsen, seek medical advice. Paediatric use: not for children under twelve as a therapeutic preparation.
Prepare each cup fresh. Dried oregano stores 12 months in a sealed tin, cool and dark.
When not to use this remedy
Known Lamiaceae sensitivity. Pregnancy at therapeutic dose. Significant breathlessness, cough with blood, chest pain, or fever over 3 days without improvement needs medical assessment.
